titleStmt.title: Letter from Fr John Fitzgibbon to Fr Provincial Thomas V. Nolan, circa April 1918
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Fitzgibbon

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from John Fitzgibbon (1882-1918) to the Father Provincial Thomas V Nolan (1867-1941) regarding missing people in Fitzgibbon's Division. Fr John 'Jack' Fitzgibbon SJ was ordained a priest on 31st July 1915. His father, John Fitzgibbon, was a successful draper and Member of Parliament from 1910-1918. Fr Fitzgibbon served in the 6th Division of the British Army, having enlisted shortly after the death of his brother at Gallipoli. He was promoted to senior chaplain in 1917, and in the same year he was gassed at Loos on 5th September and awarded the military cross. Fr Fitzgibbon was killed in action by a shell on the 18th September 1918, and is buried at Trefcon, St. Quentin, Picardie.Father Nolan was the Provincial of the Irish Province of the Society of Jesus (1912-1922) and was a member of the Distribution Committee which looked after the welfare and distribution of the the Belgian refugees who arrived in Ireland as a consequence of the First World War.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
(April 1918?) Mr Dear Fr Nolan, Our letters must
have crossed because only four
days ago I reported my existence
to you and home the very first
opportunity. Being in the
thick of it I knew all would
be anxious. In my division
four chaplains are missing,
one an R.C. a very good priest.
I escaped by only yards from
being a prisoner - & by several
miracles from being killed. Thing are a wee bit quieter
but we are desperately busy doing
all kinds of jobs & never in the
one spot 12 hours. Unfortunately
through carelessness of those behind
all of my kit is gone again & there
is no chance of getting an allowance
this time. My altar kit I had to
drop myself or I should have been
gone. Thank God I am through
so far - that is all I can say
because words are useless to describe it
As post is going. I must conclude
dear Rev Father.
With my best wishes & many thanks Your obedient child. J. Fitzgibbon. P.S
There is nothing official or even
possible re V.C> Having lost all books etc I can
only remember 10 Masses said for
Fr Farley during March. Do you
mind telling him please.
